name: Click
what_it_does: Python CLI library using decorators to create command-line interfaces. Commands are Python functions decorated with @click.command(), arguments with @click.argument(), and options with @click.option(). Supports command groups, nested subcommands, shell completion, ANSI colors, file handling, progress bars, and interactive prompts. The foundational Python CLI library (Flask same org).
use_cases: Build Python agent CLI tools with multiple subcommands using Click's @click.group() and @click.command() decorator pattern
Create interactive agent setup wizards with Click's prompt() and confirm() for guided configuration
Handle file input/output in agent CLI tools using Click's file type handling and path validation
Add shell completion to Python agent CLI tools using Click's shell completion generation
Build testable agent CLI commands using Click's CliRunner for automated CLI testing
not_for: TypeScript-first projects — Click is Python only; use Yargs or Commander for Node.js
Very simple single-argument scripts — argparse from stdlib is sufficient for trivial use
CLIs requiring type annotation-based argument parsing — Typer (built on Click) is more modern for type hint users
best_when: You're building a Python CLI agent tool with multiple commands, interactive prompts, and need battle-tested, stable foundation from the Flask ecosystem.
avoid_when: You prefer type annotation syntax over decorators — Typer provides a more modern API built on Click; or you need the absolute lightest dependency.

pricing.notes: BSD license. Maintained by Pallets organization (Flask, Jinja2, Werkzeug).

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — startup-time argument parsing only.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Type errors include expected type, received value, and option name. Missing required args name the missing option. UsageError provides user-friendly messages. CliRunner enables automated CLI testing.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: CLI argument parsing — deterministic, no side effects from framework.
known_agent_gotchas: Click decorators are applied bottom-up — the lowest decorator is applied first; argument/option order in function signature should match decorator application order for clarity
Unicode/encoding issues on Windows with click.echo() — use click.get_text_stream() or ensure UTF-8 encoding in Windows environments for non-ASCII output in agent logs
@click.pass_context requires ctx as first argument — forgetting to add ctx or mixing up parameter positions causes cryptic errors; always use is_eager=True for --help to work before other options
CliRunner in tests does not capture stderr by default — use mix_stderr=False in CliRunner and invoke(..., catch_exceptions=False) for proper test isolation of stdout/stderr
Click's confirm() prompt blocks stdin — agent tests using CliRunner must provide input via input= parameter to avoid test hangs
Context objects created by @click.pass_obj share state across nested commands — mutable context objects require careful handling in concurrent or stateful agent commands

security_score_components.security_notes: CLI framework. Passwords/secrets passed as options appear in shell history — use prompt=True, hide_input=True for password prompts rather than command-line arguments.
